LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EFREN PADILLA AND LUPE PADILLA'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO THE JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT LUPE PADILLA. WHILE IT MAY TRUE AS PLAINTIFF STATES THAT THIS IS AN "EMPTY VICTORY" FOR MS. PADILLA, THE FACTS PRESENTED ON THIS MOTION ESTABLISH THAT SHE IS ENTITLED TO THE RELIEF SHE SEEKS, EMPTY OR NOT, ON THE GROUNDS OF EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. THE PARTIES HAVE NOT BRIEFED THE ISSUE OF THE EFFECT OF MR. PADILLA'S DEATH ON THIS MOTION, SO THE COURT DECLINES TO ADDRESS THAT ISSUE, EXPLICITLY OR IMPLICITLY, AND THUS SEEKS THE PARTIES' GUIDANCE ON HOW TO PROCEED AS TO THE JUDGMENT AGAINST MR. PADILLA. IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY DECISION OTHERWISE MADE AT THE HEARING, THE MOTION AS TO MR. PADILLA WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-07-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC CARE CENTER, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO ENTER SEVERAL JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE,VACATEDEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT EFREN PADILLA AND ENTER DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST LUPE PADILLA IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 22, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE THE DEFENDANT THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 7, 2016, AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC CARE CENTER, INC.'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO ENTER SEVERAL JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT EFREN PADILLA AND ENTER DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST LUPE PADILLA IS GRANTED. ON OCTOBER 8, 2015, THE COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT LUPE PADILLA'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT JUDGMENT. A DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE SEPARATE PROCEDURES AND THE LATTER DOES NOT NECESSARILY HAVE ANY BEARING ON, AND MAY BE SET ASIDE WITHOUT DISTURBING, THE FORMER. (JONSON V. WEINSTEIN (1967) 249 CAL.APP.2D 954, 958.) HERE, THE DEFAULT AGAINST DEFENDANT LUPE PADILLA REMAINS INTACT. THE COURT VACATES THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT EFREN PADILLA AND ENTERS A DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT LUPE PADILLA. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-01-2016 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Continued To Apr-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Enter Several Judgment, Or, In The Alternative,Vacatedefault Judgment Against Defendant Efren Padilla And Enter Default Judgment Against Lupe Padilla
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PACIFIC CARE CENTER, INC.'S Motion For An Order To Enter Several Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Vacate default Judgment Against Defendant Efren Padilla And Enter Default Judgment Against Lupe Padilla is granted. On October 8, 2015, the court granted Defendant Lupe Padilla's motion for relief from default judgment. A default and default judgment are separate procedures and the latter does not necessarily have any bearing on, and may be set aside without disturbing, the former. (Jonson v. Weinstein (1967) 249 Cal.App.2d 954, 958.) Here, the default against Defendant Lupe Padilla remains intact. The court vacates the default judgment against Defendant Efren Padilla and enters a default judgment against Defendant Lupe Padilla. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Enter Several Judgment, Or, In The Alternative,Vacatedefault Judgment Against Defendant Efren Padilla And Enter Default Judgment Against Lupe Padilla
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Continued To Jan-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2015 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. J. EDWARD WILSON IV, ESQ. of Sanders Rehaste Sternshein & Harvey, LLP, Tel No. 714-289-7070 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. DIANA R. KAEMPFER, ESQ. of Bishop Law Group, APC, Tel No. 925-482-0900 appearing for Defendants Efren Padilla and Lupe Padilla.

RULING - Matter not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS EFREN PADILLA AND LUPE PADILLA'S Motion For Relief From Default Judgment is granted as to the judgment against defendant Lupe Padilla. While it may true as plaintiff states that this is an "empty victory" for Ms. Padilla, the facts presented on this motion establish that she is entitled to the relief she seeks, empty or not, on the grounds of excusable neglect. The parties have not briefed the issue of the effect of Mr. Padilla's death on this motion, so the court declines to address that issue, explicitly or implicitly, and thus seeks the parties' guidance on how to proceed as to the judgment against Mr. Padilla. In the absence of any decision otherwise made at the hearing, the motion as to Mr. Padilla will be taken off calendar. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
